applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 and later where the audit subcategory is enabled
name: Windows is starting up
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4608 records startup of LSASS and initialization of the auditing subsystem.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event is logged when LSASS.EXE starts and the auditing subsystem initializes, typically during OS startup.

key_fields.explanation: Startup timestamp for the audited system, used to anchor boot timelines.
The host whose auditing subsystem initialized.
Compare with 4609 shutdown and uptime expectations to identify unexpected reboots.

false_positives: Normal OS startup after planned maintenance or patching.
Lab and workstation reboots create routine 4608 events.
detection_notes: Use threshold=1 unexpected 4608 outside a maintenance window on servers where reboot should be rare. Microsoft documents 4608 as LSASS/auditing subsystem startup; if no matching 4609 or approved reboot exists and the host later shows cleared logs or service changes, the sequence can support T1529 System Shutdown/Reboot. Correlate TimeCreated with 4616 time changes, 1102 audit-log clears, and service-start events.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4608
| project TimeGenerated, Computer

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4608
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4608
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields documented on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4608
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4608
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source and field aliases vary by deployment.
Apply event-specific field conditions after validating local extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4608
assumptions: The applicable Windows event channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 4608
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 4608
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4608
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the documented conditions and correlations in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: Windows startup and initialization of the LSA security subsystem.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the complete event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The startup time used for reboot and audit-gap correlation.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TimeCreated with the complete event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4608 mean?
faqs.answer: Windows and the Local Security Authority security subsystem started.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 4608 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. It is expected after every normal system startup.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 4608 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Compare TimeCreated with maintenance records, boot telemetry, audit gaps, and preceding shutdown or crash evidence.
